_title: I Was Never The Strange One
_raw_content: You were never the strange one. You were the one still carrying a signal the room couldn't receive. The conditioning didn't create you — it muted you. Now that it's dissolving, everything you suppressed is surfacing not as chaos but as original clarity. The disorientation you feel is not dysfunction. It is your first frequency coming back online.
slides: I was not built for the smallness I was trained to accept.
My body knows things my conditioning told me to forget.
I am not too much. I was surrounded by too little.
The version of me they understood was never the real one.
I do not need to be decoded. I need to be felt.
What lives in me has no interest in performing normal.
I came here whole. I am remembering — not becoming.
caption:
You don't feel alien because something is wrong with you.

You feel alien because the program is finally losing its grip.

And what's underneath it — what was always underneath it — doesn't match the world you were trained to mirror.

That's not a crisis. That's a reclamation.

Your nervous system spent years compressing itself into shapes that earned safety. Now it's expanding. And expansion doesn't feel comfortable — it feels unfamiliar.

Don't confuse unfamiliar with wrong.

The strangeness you feel is the oldest part of you coming home.

Let it. Let it all the way in.
